# Test cmd2mcpserver tool definition output
#
# This test verifies that the -tool-def flag outputs valid JSON

# Create a test program with various flag types
-- testcli/main.go --
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
)

var (
	name    = flag.String("name", "world", "name to greet")
	count   = flag.Int("count", 1, "number of times to greet")
	verbose = flag.Bool("verbose", false, "enable verbose output")
	timeout = flag.Float64("timeout", 30.0, "timeout in seconds")
)

func main() {
	flag.Parse()
	for i := 0; i < *count; i++ {
		if *verbose {
			fmt.Printf("[%d/%d] ", i+1, *count)
		}
		fmt.Printf("Hello, %s!\n", *name)
	}
}

-- testcli/go.mod --
module github.com/test/testcli

go 1.21

# Run cmd2mcpserver with -tool-def flag
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -tool-def testcli
stdout '{'
stdout '"name":\s*"testcli"'
stdout '"description":\s*"MCP wrapper'
stdout '"inputSchema":'
stdout '"type":\s*"object"'
stdout '"properties":'

# Check that all flag types are correctly represented
stdout '"name":\s*{\s*"default":\s*"world"'
stdout '"type":\s*"string"'
stdout '"count":\s*{\s*"default":\s*"1"'
stdout '"type":\s*"integer"'
stdout '"verbose":\s*{\s*"default":\s*false'
stdout '"type":\s*"boolean"'
stdout '"timeout":\s*{\s*"default":\s*"30"'
stdout '"type":\s*"number"'

# Verify it's valid JSON by using jq if available
exec sh -c 'go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -tool-def testcli | jq . > /dev/null || true'

# Test with source directory
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -tool-def -source testcli testcli
stdout '"name":\s*{\s*"default":\s*"world"'
stdout '"description":\s*"name to greet"'

# Check for return type
stdout '"returnType":'
stdout '"stdout":\s*{'
stdout '"stderr":\s*{'
stdout '"returnCode":\s*{'
stdout '"type":\s*"integer"'
stdout '"description":\s*"Exit code of the command"'
